description: CPI instrument signals mapped to AXIOLEV MASTER v3.1 instruments. CPI does not overwrite existing instrument scores; it provides diagnostic signal about model-layer capability gaps.

cpi_signals.note: Semantic retrieval precision — core I1 signal

I1_perception_lexicon.interpretation: I1=88.80 is measured from AXIOLEV corpus (NVIR lexicon, canon alignment). CPI A1 shows weak multi-needle retrieval — indicates model layer can't reliably surface embedded semantic signals. Faithfulness=100 prevents lower bound. Gap is context retrieval, not grounding.
I1_perception_lexicon.actionable: Prompt engineering: structured retrieval cues. Or: RAG over NVIR corpus.

I2_reasoning.interpretation: I2=89.74 is measured via NVIR evolutionary candidates and CPS op correctness. CPI C1 calibration=46.5% reveals model overconfidence and factual error (17 is prime) — a direct I2 threat. Reasoning density=3.8% low. The AXIOLEV I2 score reflects NS-layer reasoning; the model layer has a calibration gap that NVIR partially compensates.
I2_reasoning.actionable: Fine-tune calibration: penalize overconfidence. Add system-prompt uncertainty instruction.

cpi_signals.note: Working context window — primary I3 signal

I3_memory_coherence.interpretation: MECW=100% is strong — model retrieves target across 8000-token contexts. Multi-needle weakness (37.5%) indicates that when multiple competing items coexist, coherence degrades. I3 score is well-supported by Alexandria SSD + receipt chain, which compensate for model-layer context noise.
I3_memory_coherence.actionable: Chained retrieval: decompose multi-needle to sequential single-needle queries.

cpi_signals.note: Execution throughput — direct I4 performance signal

I4_synthesis_execution.interpretation: I4=97.88 already near ceiling. CPI performance confirms: 0.141s avg, p95=0.170s, 71 tps proxy. No contradiction. Model layer execution is non-bottleneck. NVIR credits dominate I4; model latency contribution is positive.
I4_synthesis_execution.actionable: None required. Monitor if model changes.

I5_sovereignty_memory.interpretation: Abstention=100% is excellent — model correctly refuses unanswerable questions, aligned with Dignity Kernel. Position bias=66.7% (recency failure) is a memory sovereignty risk: model cannot retrieve facts placed at end of context. I5 is supported by Alexandria + TierLatch; model-layer I5 signal is net positive (abstention dominates).
I5_sovereignty_memory.actionable: For recency: place high-priority facts at context start or repeat at end.

summary.gap_analysis: CPI reveals model-layer weaknesses partially masked by NS-layer infrastructure (NVIR, Alexandria, Receipt Chain). These gaps are real but do not open new safe path to Omega-Certified (93.0) — gap remains structural in I1/I5 perception/sovereignty capability.
